Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Complex conditional clause; nominal subject + future auxiliary 'ghá' + verb phrase
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: Ìrù má zẹ̀ ẹ̀, ó má gha dé ìkpá.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common Benin court-style advisory proverb emphasizing bravery in communal advancement
First Example Sentence: Wẹ́ má gha sẹ̀, ùhúnmwùn erù, ọ̀nà ghá kpá.
Second Example Sentence: Òré, yá ẹ̀ sẹ̀; ùhúnmwùn erù, ọ̀nà ghá kpá.
Third Example Sentence: Nà ìgbìnmwìn nà, ùhúnmwùn erù, ọ̀nà ghá kpá.

Idiom (Edo): Obọ̀ ghá fi ẹ̀rù ré ogba
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Future construction; subject + auxiliary 'ghá' + serial verb phrase
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: Ẹ̀rù rẹ̀ gha yé vbe ẹ̀kà.
Usage Contexts: Justice discourse, family correction, moral teaching
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Agrarian imagery referencing farms (ogba) where concealment is impossible
First Example Sentence: Wẹ́ gha mọ̀, obọ̀ ghá fi ẹ̀rù ré ogba.
Second Example Sentence: Àgbà rọ̀ wẹ́ pé obọ̀ ghá fi ẹ̀rù ré ogba.
Third Example Sentence: Obọ̀ ghá fi ẹ̀rù ré ogba, ó gha yé.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Nominal subject + future auxiliary + transitive verb; metaphorical object
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: Ọ̀rọ̀ má gha kpá ènìyàn kpẹ̀.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Refers to splitting bean cake (okpa) symbolizing division
First Example Sentence: Má yé ọ̀rọ̀ rhie; ọ̀rọ̀ ghá yá okpa.
Second Example Sentence: Vbe meeting nà, ọ̀rọ̀ ghá yá okpa.
Third Example Sentence: Àgbà gha rọ̀ pé ọ̀rọ̀ ghá yá okpa.

Idiom (Edo): Ọmọ̀ má ghá mọ̀ ọ̀nà kó má bọ̀ ìhó
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative conditional construction with subjunctive nuance
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: Ọmọ̀ má gha dé ẹ̀rù nà nà.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional teaching proverb about child development
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Edo): /ɔ̀mɔ̀ má ɣá mọ̀ ɔ̀nà kò má bɔ̀ ìhó/
First Example Sentence: Má kpá ọmọ̀ nà; ọmọ̀ má ghá mọ̀ ọ̀nà kó má bọ̀ ìhó.
Second Example Sentence: Àgbà yé pé ọmọ̀ má ghá mọ̀ ọ̀nà kó má bọ̀ ìhó.
Third Example Sentence: Vbe ilé nà, ọmọ̀ má ghá mọ̀ ọ̀nà kó má bọ̀ ìhó.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Simple future clause; metaphorical subject-object structure
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Refers to excessive grabbing (èkpètin) disrupting harmony
First Example Sentence: Wẹ́ má gha kpá owó rhie; èkpètin ghá kpá èkhoe.
Second Example Sentence: Èkpètin ghá kpá èkhoe vbe ilé nà.
Third Example Sentence: Àgbà gha rọ̀ pé èkpètin ghá kpá èkhoe.

First Example Sentence: Yá ẹ̀ sẹ̀; ọ̀nà má ghá zẹ̀ sẹ̀sẹ̀.
Second Example Sentence: Vbe school nà, ọ̀nà má ghá zẹ̀ sẹ̀sẹ̀.
Third Example Sentence: Ọ̀nà má ghá zẹ̀ sẹ̀sẹ̀, ṣùgbọ́n yá ẹ̀.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Future intransitive clause with locative phrase
First Example Sentence: Má gha sẹ̀; ọ̀kpọ̀ ghá yé vbe ẹ̀kà.
Second Example Sentence: Ọ̀kpọ̀ ghá yé vbe ẹ̀kà rhie.
Third Example Sentence: Àgbà rọ̀ pé ọ̀kpọ̀ ghá yé vbe ẹ̀kà.

First Example Sentence: Yá ẹ̀ sẹ̀; èrù má ghá kpá ogbè.
Second Example Sentence: Èrù má ghá kpá ogbè vbe ògió.
Third Example Sentence: Àgbà gha rọ̀ pé èrù má ghá kpá ogbè.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative future clause; metaphorical verb phrase
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Harsh words do not defeat wisdom
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: Ọ̀rọ̀ rẹ̀ má gha kpá ènìyàn tí ó yé.
First Example Sentence: Wẹ́ má gha fi ọ̀rọ̀ kpá mí; ọ̀rọ̀ má ghá kpá àgbà.
Second Example Sentence: Vbe palace nà, ọ̀rọ̀ má ghá kpá àgbà.
Third Example Sentence: Ọ̀rọ̀ má ghá kpá àgbà, àgbà gha rọ̀.

Idiom (Edo): Èrhàn ghá wé, àmẹn ghá yé
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Parallel future clause construction; coordinated metaphor
First Example Sentence: Wẹ́ gha mọ̀, èrhàn ghá wé, àmẹn ghá yé.
Second Example Sentence: Èrhàn ghá wé, àmẹn ghá yé vbe ìlú nà.
Third Example Sentence: Àgbà gha sẹ̀ pé èrhàn ghá wé, àmẹn ghá yé.
